reviews.author: Aniket Todankar
reviews.text: Cát Bà was absolutely stunning. The cruise experience was excellent, with breathtaking views at every turn and landscapes that felt straight out of a postcard. Calm waters, towering limestone cliffs, and changing shades of green made the journey truly mesmerizing. What stayed with me most was Cát Bà Island itself. It is incredible to see locals living there despite how remote and isolated it feels. The simplicity of life against such raw natural beauty makes the place even more special and unforgettable.

reviews.author: Irene Krajewska
reviews.text: We started at the museum. Nice old building. No staff but left open to wander through. Signage all in Vietnamese but Google translate is very useful. Some nice photos of what you may (rarely) see in the park. Some old jars with animal specimens in. Then we walked along a newly concreted path into the forest. Past the sign to a village and frog pond to an eel pond which we never saw. Then the road just stopped. In the middle of nowhere. Then we walked back out. 6.5km round trip. Up and down steep hills. One area off the road was a previously flooded limestone field which we explored and found old dried fishing nets containing small dead fish from in the flood.

reviews.author: Iulia-Agnana Atomoaei
reviews.text: An Unforgettable Jungle Escape My visit to Cat Ba National Park yesterday was absolutely amazing. The trek through the lush, wild jungle to Ngu Lam Peak was a real adventure, and the panoramic 360-degree view from the top was breathtaking and worth every step. The serene, pristine atmosphere of this UNESCO biosphere reserve is truly special. A perfect five-star experience for any nature lover.

reviews.author: Sara
reviews.text: I think this tripp would have been better without the group and guide, just hike on your own and take your time to look at things you want. There is a building with a lot of stuffed animals very interesting and then there are rescue animals to the right when you enter.

reviews.author: Chris Clemens
reviews.text: 🌿 Cat Ba National Park – Amazing Experience! 🌿 Cat Ba National Park was absolutely amazing. The hike up to Ngu Lam Peak was a bit tough at times, but 100% worth the effort. The views from the top were out of this world – lush green mountains, deep valleys, and scenery that feels untouched. If you’re visiting Cat Ba Island and enjoy nature or hiking, this is a must-do. Just bring water, good shoes, and be ready for a climb. Totally unforgettable experience!

reviews.author: Hann G
reviews.text: The park is absolutely beautiful. We did the long hike and it was totally worth it. I just want to mention that a road has been made, and the first kms of the long hike are through the road. I really hope no other road will be made as the impact on the beautiful forest would be devastating. Also they left a lot of plastic and it would be really necessary for the environment to be taken away. A part from that I really recommend it. Definitely one of the highlights of the island.

reviews.author: Luisa Pichlmuller
reviews.text: It's possible to hike the trek alone without a guide to Viet Hai Village, but the path is challenging, good shoes, fitness and hiking experience would be good, some bits need to be climbed and throughout thick jungle, watch daylight hours! Takes about 2-3 hours. Ask the woman by the first restaurant after the path ends for a boat taxi back to cat ba, her sister will come pick you up in a small old fisher boat at the harbour 4km away from the village (1 hour walk), best experience to see the iconic islands and floating fisher village without some overcrowded tourist boat tour.

reviews.author: Steve Ozark
reviews.text: Amazing! But the 12 km hike is NOT moderate. Scaling ricks on all fours, steep drops and challenging for this 65-year old. 10 kilo backpack was a big mistake. Hey, I made it but had a few days of recovery for a semi in shape old guy.

reviews.author: Andrzej Lubiewski
reviews.text: 25th November Entrance fee 160k vnd/person There is a toilet near the entrance to the park Short trail: it took us 1hour to finish it a trail (both ways). Just before trail starts to climb up there is a restaurant/shop where you can buy water, snacks etc Long trail: for first two kilometres from the gate there is a construction site and a new sidewalk, after that jungle path begins. It took us 3 hours from the park gate to the village (without navy peak). We did few short water and photo breaks. Scrambling begins after the frog lake (more like a big puddle of dirty water). Don’t expect many landscape views, you mostly walk surrounded by jungle. In the village you can drink or eat something, all the spots were empty. Bike rent 40k/person. Bikes are old and barley working, but there were enough to finish this short ride. On the pier there is a noisy lady which offers you a boat trip back to Ben Beo - negotiate price. It’s some kind of side job for boat companies, you will be placed on a boat which heads back to city.

reviews.author: Samantha Reid
reviews.text: Always love to support national parks. The climb to the peak looked abit steep for me but plenty of others were heading up and its only 1km according to the signs. The specimen rooms were cool and freaky. We walked a couple of kms towards the village and turned back as we were never going to actually make the whole 12kms. That walk is well concreted but steep. Well worth the look. Ticket for the day as sightseeing also includes the Trung trang cave down the road.

reviews.author: Andrew Ruttle
reviews.text: Nice little walk, 120k D for entry fee, witch give u access to cave down the road , dong tong trung a cool unique cave walk ... Nice little hike up uneven surface steps... A great morning hike ... Small bird zoo also in park ...

reviews.author: Milan Marecek
reviews.text: Beautiful sceneries and nature. Treks might be tricky -very steep and sometimes harder terrain. If you are in at least average health condition, definitely worth of it.

reviews.author: Ian Redmond
reviews.text: Stunning scenery and a delight for botanists and geologists but very few birds and no mammals or reptiles seen. Nice jumping spider on the path...

reviews.author: eunique morris
reviews.text: The bat cave was a really cool experience. Wish we would have had more time to explore but we were with a group tour. There is a small store with toilets once you finish the cave.

reviews.author: Sim Sim
reviews.text: This is realy dead! This park probably was nice…. A long time agoo. It’s overgrown, sad, desolate, not maintained. Even the entrance fee of 240.000 vnd is too high for this. Just close it and forget about it. Well the ‘forgetting’ has been done allready in many ways; forgot to maintain and forgot to close. Very sad experiance. Let the animals go free (just not the snake😣) they will be better that way then living out their sad lives in those sad cages. The cave a bit further on the road is included in the price, that makes up a bit for the price.

reviews.author: shao weixing
reviews.text: The tour guide is very nice and skillful. His name is Anh and he can edit the video and make a very good video tearing the tracking break time, even though it was not included in the, and he support whole journey. He will provide a lot of things if you do not have for example like some Band-Aid and also give enough time and let you to enjoy the whole journey. the national Park is amazing you can see different landscape. the jungle the rock road, some very narrow street, muddy road and the pond different kind of animal insect. For example, a lot of spider and even snake. The food also amazing and valuable, and you can refill all your energy in that village After that, you will do the cycling very beautiful scenery along the road. then we'll take a boat. The boat will go through a very long way and you enjoy the scenery along the way you won't feel the time you won't feel the distance because it's too beautiful And we also did the kayaking, which was great and then swimming also amazing. Encourage you to join this trip.

reviews.author: Geoffrey Moore
reviews.text: This should be your first destination on the island. Climb to the top of the highest vantage point on the island in about 40 minutes with a few breaks, then descend in 23 minutes. 120k entry includes the vantage point and the Trang Trung Cave down the street. The Cave is much easier to walk than I was expecting. Neither spot shows the sea surrounding the island, but thats because the surrounding mountains are numerous and almost equally as tall as this one. The greenery never ends. The juice is worth the squeeze.

reviews.author: Katleen Verloo
reviews.text: Good for an hour or 2! Hike was not too difficult, any able bodied person can do it. We did it in 45 minutes (me and 2 kids) and there were loads of other children. Bring water as you cannot buy any after the start of the walk (there is a small restaurant at the start, but nothing after that). Long sleeves or trousers not needed I'd say (too hot) but mosquito repellent definitely yes. Beautiful view over the surrounding area. The entry fee is low and includes Trung Trang cave a little further down the road (1 km). There is a small museum with creepy animals, too. Kids loved those ;-)

reviews.author: Sneh K
reviews.text: Wanted to hike Navy tower but reached late. Went to national park leak instead, it was scenic panorama at the top. Not much crowded as it was weekday

reviews.author: Chris Bowman
reviews.text: Incredible sights, over-crowded with tour boats. Bucket-list location. Enjoyed tours to pearl farm, fishing village and caverns (but felt guilty adding to the overcrowding). Glad we took the three day cruise

reviews.author: Jan Schober
reviews.text: Of you want to enter the Walking area, tot need to buy a ticket (120.000), it is also the entrance fee for the caves 1km from the entrance. The park is old, but the nature is beautiful, the "zoo" has some rabbits, Ducks and other easy animals, they don't look happy. The walk to the top is steep, hot and slippery, but real Vietnam jungle and worth it.

reviews.author: Servé Dierdorp
reviews.text: This is a wonderful national park, I would love to go back one day. It's stunning and I enjoyed it as one of my favourite places in Vietnam.

reviews.author: prajwal kulkarni
reviews.text: The trek takes about an hour to reach the top. The trekking experience in itself was good but the view in the end didn’t feel much rewarding personally. +1 for keeping the park free from trash.

reviews.author: Chris Waldren
reviews.text: We were able to spend a night on the boat and visited several caves, the boat was a great experience.

reviews.author: Rico Trebeljahr
reviews.text: Beautiful Carst mountains and caves :) A bit touristic but worth it anyways.

reviews.author: Karl Ward
reviews.text: Fantastic place. Lovely tour by bike to a village filled with dogs and if you're very lucky then you'll see the monkeys.

reviews.author: John Chrysostom
reviews.text: We were at this national park the other day. It was a rainy day, hence walking and climbing was not easy as some areas were slippery. Nice view and a nice and peaceful place. The museum was also nice and informative.

reviews.author: Swantje Ide
reviews.text: Please make a hiking tour, the national park is really beautiful

reviews.author: steve lazari
reviews.text: The trail to Ngu Lam peak was really nice. Entrance fee is a bit high, although the nearby cave is included in the price of the ticket as well and the views from the top are beautiful. It is about 45 minutes to the top, and quite strenuous, so I wouldn’t recommend for those looking for an easy trail

reviews.author: Wisse
reviews.text: Nice little hike! If your in the area and its a bright day you should defiantly do it!
